name: October CMS PHP API
what_it_does: Laravel-based open-source CMS with REST API capabilities — enabling PHP developers to build content-managed websites with a simple, plugin-based architecture, database-backed content, drag-and-drop page builder, and API-driven headless delivery via the October CMS REST API plugin, combining Laravel's power with a marketer-friendly admin interface.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via October CMS REST API plugin for decoupled frontends
Managing October CMS pages and blog posts via API for content automation
Building custom API endpoints in October CMS via Laravel controllers for specialized content delivery
Managing October CMS users and backend settings via API for CMS provisioning
Integrating October CMS content with external systems via REST API for data synchronization
not_for: Teams without PHP/Laravel expertise (October CMS extends Laravel)
Teams needing managed SaaS CMS without server administration
Enterprise-scale deployments requiring multi-tenant or FedRAMP compliance
best_when: A PHP/Laravel developer or agency needs a straightforward CMS that balances developer control with marketer-friendly editing — with Laravel's ecosystem, a plugin marketplace, and simple drag-and-drop page builder.
avoid_when: You don't have PHP/Laravel expertise, need managed SaaS, or require advanced compliance certifications.

auth.notes: REST API via October CMS API plugin (official or third-party). API key auth via plugin. Laravel Sanctum for token-based API auth in v3. No OAuth2 out-of-box. No webhooks natively — use custom Event hooks in Laravel. PHP expertise required for custom endpoints. v3 (current) uses Laravel 10 — modern auth patterns available via Sanctum.

pricing.free_tier_limits: October CMS Community Edition: free and open source (MIT)
pricing.paid_tiers: October CMS Professional: $99/year per project; Enterprise: custom
pricing.notes: Canadian company. Created by Sam Geron and Alexey Bobkov in 2014. MIT license (Community Edition). Annual license for Professional features (advanced support, premium plugins). GitHub: octobercms/october (11,000+ stars). Built on Laravel 10 (v3). MySQL/PostgreSQL backend. Plugin marketplace with 900+ plugins. Used by agencies for client websites. Simple and approachable for PHP developers. Marketer-friendly backend. v3 (2022) was major rewrite — upgraded to Laravel 10, improved API.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API via plugin — must install separately. Laravel Sanctum for v3 token auth. No webhooks natively. PHP/Laravel expertise required. Self-hosted. No rate limits. Open source. Plugin quality varies. v3 is major rewrite — check for v2 vs v3 API differences.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads idempotent by ID. Page reads idempotent by slug. Write operations idempotent by record ID. No built-in idempotency keys.
known_agent_gotchas: No built-in REST API — must install plugin (official or community)
No webhooks natively — use Laravel Events/Observers for hooks
v2 vs v3 are significantly different — check API version in use
Plugin quality varies in marketplace — evaluate before production use
Canadian company — smaller community than WordPress/Drupal

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on server config. User-controlled data residency. Laravel Sanctum token auth (v3). No OAuth2 out-of-box. Open source — auditable Laravel code. Laravel's security model (CSRF, XSS protection). User responsible for server hardening. Plugin security depends on plugin quality. No compliance certifications.
